content.body: Polymers are large molecules made of repeating monomer units. They are found in nature (proteins, DNA, cellulose) and are synthesised industrially (plastics, fibres).

Addition polymerisation:
nCH₂=CH₂ → (-CH₂-CH₂-)ₙ  (poly(ethene))

Condensation polymerisation:
Monomer₁ + Monomer₂ → Polymer + small molecule (H₂O or HCl)

Nylon (polyamide): amine + carboxylic acid → amide bond + H₂O
PET (polyester): diol + dicarboxylic acid → ester bond + H₂O

Biodegradable polymers: Polylactic acid (PLA), PHB

content.title: Addition vs Condensation
content.body:
Addition: All atoms in monomers end up in polymer
Poly(ethene): CH₂=CH₂ → (-CH₂CH₂-)ₙ

Condensation: Small molecule lost per bond formed
Nylon-6,6: H₂N-(CH₂)₆-NH₂ + HOOC-(CH₂)₄-COOH
→ (-NH-(CH₂)₆-NH-CO-(CH₂)₄-CO-)ₙ + H₂O

questions.question: Addition polymerisation requires monomers with:
questions.explanation: Addition polymers form from alkene monomers whose double bonds open to join together

questions.question: Condensation polymerisation always produces a polymer and:
questions.explanation: Condensation reactions eliminate a small molecule (H₂O or HCl) for each bond formed

questions.question: Nylon is a type of:
questions.explanation: Nylon contains amide (-CO-NH-) linkages, making it a polyamide

questions.question: Which polymer is biodegradable?
questions.explanation: PLA is derived from corn starch and breaks down in composting conditions

questions.question: The repeating unit in poly(propene) is:
questions.explanation: Poly(propene) is made from propene (CH₂=CHCH₃), giving the repeat unit -CH₂-CH(CH₃)-
